=== CRYPTO PORTFOLIO RUN — 2026-07-01 ===  (data: TradingView MCP)

BTC's MACD histogram crashed from +5.89 to -55.06 overnight — the sharpest single-session momentum reversal of this cycle — confirming the downtrend is deepening rather than bottoming. Spot bitcoin ETFs shed $4.5 billion in June, their worst month on record since launching in January 2024, with BlackRock's IBIT alone responsible for $3.55 billion of those outflows. ETH bucked the trend with its MACD turning positive for the first time in weeks (+2.42 from -20.81), though its RSI at 34 keeps it on hold. AAVE held its buy signal despite a 6.5% drop ($89.49 → $85.74) — still above its short-term moving average at $82.04 and generating diversified real revenue. SOL closed the gap further: now just 1.1% below its EMA20 trigger at $75.43 — one daily close above that level upgrades to a buy. JUP momentum improved sharply (RSI jumped from 52 to 60), reinforcing the small buy. Fear & Greed fell from 15 to 11 — Extreme Fear deepened. Taiwan passed the Virtual Asset Service Act today, a constructive long-run regulatory signal for the industry.

=== BLOCK 2 — Per-Token Verdicts ===

### BTC — HOLD
BTC slipped to $58,605, deeper below its 200-week cost-basis floor ($62,640). The most alarming development: MACD histogram crashed from +5.89 to -55.06 in a single session — the sharpest momentum reversal of this cycle, confirming the downtrend is accelerating rather than stabilizing. RSI fell further to 30.22. June ETF outflows of $4.5 billion set an all-time monthly record, driven by macro rotation and the SpaceX IPO capital drain. No bullish trigger present. HOLD.

Research Desk:
  Technical:   $58,605. Far below EMA20 $66,537 and 200wMA $62,640. Death cross active. RSI 30.22 (deepening). MACD hist -55.06 (was +5.89 — massive single-session flip). -38.7% from 52w high.
  On-Chain:    93.6% of 200wMA ($62,640) — below the historical cost-basis floor for the second day [source: TradingView data]. Closes below 200wMA have historically marked deep capitulation zones; no confirmed reversal signal.
  DeFi:        n/a — L1 (no protocol revenue accrual to token holders)
  Macro:       Spot bitcoin ETFs shed $4.5 billion in June — worst month since January 2024 launch. BlackRock IBIT alone shed $3.55 billion. Nine-day outflow streak, $222.6M on June 30 alone. Analysts cite macro rotation and SpaceX IPO as primary drivers [source: https://www.theblock.co/post/406802/bitcoin-etf-worst-month-yet]. Taiwan passed the Virtual Asset Service Act today — licensing required from FSC, a constructive long-run institutional signal [source: https://www.theblock.co/post/406816/taiwan-passes-crypto-law].
  Smart Money: Strategy buyback program ongoing; Ark Invest loaded crypto-adjacent equities (Coinbase, Circle, Robinhood) — indirect accumulation signal, not direct BTC [source: https://www.theblock.co/post/406628/ark-invest-coinbase-circle-bullish-robinhood-monday].

Panel:
  Graham (Value):          NEUTRAL — 93.6% of 200wMA; not cheap enough (<80%) to clear a margin-of-safety threshold; no cash-flow anchor.
  Buffett (Quality):       NEUTRAL — dominant monetary network moat but no revenue to underwrite a buy.
  Dalio (Cycle):           BEARISH — ETF outflows accelerating ($4.5B June), hawkish Fed, strong DXY = textbook risk-off/tightening trigger.
  Druckenmiller (Trend):   BEARISH — RSI 30.22 < 40 ✗, death cross active ✗, MACD hist -55.06 (catastrophic single-day reversal from +5.89); downtrend sharply accelerating.
  Burniske (On-chain):     NEUTRAL — no protocol fee capture; valued purely on monetary premium.

Bull: 93.6% of 200wMA in Extreme Fear (F&G 11) — historically one of the highest-reward zones for patient capital at cycle lows.
Bear: MACD hist -55.06 is the worst momentum reading of this cycle; ETF outflow record set in June; no liquidity or macro catalyst to form a bottom.

At 63.6% of its 200-week average, ETH remains deeply discounted with real fee/burn value and Bitmine absorbing 4.7% of supply. Three bulls, two bears — split — hold.

UK FCA framework (Oct 2027) carries over as institutional clarity catalyst [source: https://www.theblock.co/post/406546/uk-sets-capital-market-abuse-rules-landmark-crypto-framework].
  Smart Money: Bitmine holds 5.70M ETH (4.7% of supply, $8.9B) — structural float tightener [source: https://www.theblock.co/post/406538/bitmine-lifts-ethereum-treasury-to-5-7-million-eth-through-challenging-weekly-slide-joins-russell-1000].   Burniske (On-chain):     BULLISH — service fees + token buyback (Payment Abstraction) accrue to holders.

Bull: Best fundamental moat in the book (Swift/JPM/UBS) at a deep discount — extremely high quality at the wrong moment of the cycle.
Bear: RSI < 40 and worsening MACD — Druckenmiller BEARISH gate met; SPLIT holds until trend turns.

=== TELEGRAM RECAP ===

📊 *Crypto Portfolio — July 1, 2026*

Bitcoin's momentum indicator just had its worst single-day reading of this entire cycle, and June turned out to be the worst month ever for bitcoin investment funds (outflows of $4.5 billion). The market is getting worse before it gets better — but that's where buying opportunities often show up.

**Two buys remain open:**
🟢 **AAVE** — fell 6.5% over two days to $85.74 but is still holding above its short-term support at $82. All momentum conditions are still satisfied. The pullback is actually a slightly better entry price. Size in modestly; a close below $82 would invalidate.
🟢 **JUP (small position)** — momentum picked up sharply today (momentum score jumped from 52 to 60). Still a small-size position given limited track record.

**Closest to joining:**
⚡ **SOL** — just 1.1% below its moving average trigger at $75.43. A close above that today or tomorrow upgrades it to a buy.

**On watch:**
• ETH's momentum indicator turned positive for the first time in weeks — an early sign that sellers may be slowing, though price is still falling.
• Taiwan passed a crypto licensing law today — a constructive long-term signal.
